repo_notes.content: Generate wiki pages from the current repository state, not from assumed upstream behavior. Prefer README.md, docs/tech-notes, docs/dev-notes, docs/plans, and concrete files under lib/ and test/ as evidence.

For every page, explain ownership boundaries explicitly: identify which surface is canonical authority, which surfaces are legacy compatibility or migration bridges, and which surfaces are UI-only or diagnostic-only.

Do not flatten the repo into a generic Flutter app summary. This fork is in active migration, so pages should call out where lib/features is replacing older logic in lib/pages, lib/foundation, or utils.
Use concrete file and directory names in the generated wiki text. Point readers to the main entrypoints, owner modules, and the highest-signal tests for each subsystem.
Do not overclaim centralization or completion. If a subsystem is mid-cutover, say that clearly and name the current bridge, fallback, or residual legacy surface.
Treat diagnostics and tests as part of the architecture contract. When a page covers routing, persistence, or cutover behavior, mention the guard tests or trace contracts that enforce that behavior.

Keep the wiki focused on how this fork works today: startup, routing, storage, source runtime, reader runtime, migration/cutover lanes, and docs/tracker surfaces. Avoid low-signal restatements of obvious Flutter concepts.

pages.title: Overview
pages.purpose: Introduce the Venera fork, explain the breaking-change direction, summarize the main subsystem boundaries, and orient readers to the rest of the wiki.

page_notes.content: Use README.md as the primary source. Explain that this is a maintained fork of an archived upstream project and that fragmented legacy local data layouts are not treated as a permanent compatibility target.

pages.title: Setup & Bootstrap
pages.purpose: Document how to run the app and how process startup flows into the runtime bootstrap and App singleton.

page_notes.content: Cover lib/main.dart, lib/headless.dart, lib/init.dart, lib/utils/init.dart, and the current Flutter/tooling baseline from README.md. Explain the boot path clearly instead of listing dependencies mechanically.

pages.title: Fork Direction & Active Roadmap
pages.purpose: Explain the architectural migration direction, what the fork is replacing, and where active planning and tracking live.

page_notes.content: Use docs/plans/tracker.md plus recent active plans under docs/plans/. Distinguish long-term targets from work that is still in progress, and mention docs/plans/done/ and docs/plans/old/ as historical context rather than current authority.

pages.title: Core Architecture & App Lifecycle
pages.purpose: Describe app-wide runtime ownership: the App singleton, initialization lifecycle, global state, and cross-feature foundation surfaces.

page_notes.content: Focus on lib/foundation/app/app.dart, lib/foundation/app/global_state.dart, lib/init.dart, and nearby owner modules. Make the page about ownership and lifecycle, not just class names.

pages.title: Navigation & Routing Ownership
pages.purpose: Explain adaptive navigation, route construction, AppRouter ownership, AppPageRoute behavior, and the fork’s routing authority model.

page_notes.content: Use lib/app/router.dart, lib/pages/main_page.dart, lib/foundation/app/app_page_route.dart, lib/foundation/adaptive/app_window_class.dart, and docs/tech-notes/routing-model-storage-diagnostics-ownership.md. Emphasize the move toward one route owner for critical flows.

pages.title: Settings, Appdata & Configuration
pages.purpose: Document Appdata, typed settings, runtime preferences, and the current split between configuration state and domain authority.

page_notes.content: Use lib/foundation/appdata.dart, lib/pages/settings/, docs/plans/2026-05-03-appdata-owner-decisions.md, and related tests. Be explicit about what still lives in appdata and what should not remain domain authority.

pages.title: Diagnostics, Logging & Debugging
pages.purpose: Describe the structured diagnostics stack, legacy logging surfaces, debug export, lifecycle guards, and debugging conventions.

page_notes.content: Use lib/foundation/diagnostics/diagnostics.dart, debug_log_exporter.dart, app_lifecycle_guard.dart, lib/foundation/reader/reader_diagnostics.dart, and the relevant diagnostics tests. Treat diagnostics as behavior contracts, not optional observability garnish.

pages.title: Source Plugin System Overview
pages.purpose: Explain the JavaScript-based source/plugin architecture, its main boundaries, and how source runtime ownership is organized.

page_notes.content: Orient readers across lib/features/sources/comic_source/, lib/foundation/js/, and lib/network/. Make clear which surfaces are source-runtime owners versus shared runtime utilities.

pages.title: Source Models, Parser & JS Runtime
pages.purpose: Describe source models, parser handoff, validation, and the QuickJS-backed runtime bridge.

page_notes.content: Use comic_source.dart, comic_source_parser.dart, direct_js_parser_handoff.dart, direct_js_source_validator.dart, lib/foundation/js/js_engine.dart, and the corresponding tests. Show how parsing and execution are separated.

pages.title: Source Auth, WebView & Account Data
pages.purpose: Explain per-source authentication, cookies, login flows, and persisted account/session surfaces.

page_notes.content: Use lib/pages/auth_page.dart, lib/pages/webview.dart, lib/network/cookie_jar.dart, and source account/config code. Keep cookie/session storage distinct from canonical comic-domain state.

pages.title: Source Management & Direct Install
pages.purpose: Document source repository registry work, staged source writing, validation/install flow, and the current boundary between enabled and unfinished install behavior.

page_notes.content: Use source_management_controller.dart, direct_js_install_command.dart, direct_js_staged_source_writer.dart, source_management_redaction.dart, and the source_management tests. Be explicit if UI surfaces are validate-only or staged-write-only.

pages.title: Data Layer & Persistence Overview
pages.purpose: Map canonical and legacy storage surfaces, ports/adapters, authority boundaries, and current persistence direction.

page_notes.content: Use lib/foundation/db/, lib/foundation/database/, lib/foundation/ports/, lib/foundation/repositories/, and docs/dev-notes/app_db_helper_write_audit.md. This page should frame the storage model before deeper child pages.

pages.title: AppDbHelper Write Serialization
pages.purpose: Explain the canonical runtime write entry boundary, write queue semantics, transaction/customWrite helpers, label conventions, and retry behavior.

page_notes.content: Use lib/foundation/database/app_db_helper.dart, docs/dev-notes/app_db_helper_write_audit.md, test/foundation/database/app_db_helper_test.dart, and test/foundation/db/app_db_helper_wiring_grep_test.dart. Clarify what counts as normal runtime writes versus setup/migration exceptions.

pages.title: UnifiedComicsStore & Repository Adapters
pages.purpose: Describe the canonical Drift/SQLite store, split schema files, WAL/foreign-key setup, record types, and adapters that expose store behavior to features.

page_notes.content: Use lib/foundation/db/unified_comics_store.dart, the split files under lib/foundation/db/unified_comics_store/, and lib/foundation/db/adapters/. Explain why this store is the canonical comic-domain authority.

pages.title: Reader Sessions, Tabs & Progress Persistence
pages.purpose: Document reader session records, tab records, active-tab behavior, persistence orchestration, and write/skip diagnostics.

page_notes.content: Use lib/features/reader/data/reader_session_repository.dart, reader_session_persistence.dart, lib/foundation/db/unified_comics_store.dart, and tests around reader persistence. Explain DB-authoritative correctness versus repository fast-path coalescing.

pages.title: Source Identity & Platform Resolver
pages.purpose: Explain canonical source/platform identity, alias mapping, legacy key bridging, and the sourceRef/sourceIdentity model used by storage and routing.

page_notes.content: Use lib/foundation/sources/identity/, lib/foundation/sources/source_ref.dart, and source resolver tests. Make this page about identity ownership, not just enum/value listings.

pages.title: Local Library, History & Favorites Authority
pages.purpose: Explain how local-library, history, favorites, follow-updates, and reading progress currently span canonical and compatibility surfaces.

page_notes.content: Use lib/foundation/local/, local.dart, history.dart, favorites.dart, favorite_runtime_authority.dart, follow_updates.dart, and related tests. Be explicit about which parts are still bridges and which parts are canonical owners.

pages.title: Networking, Proxy & Caching
pages.purpose: Describe the app-wide networking stack, request configuration, proxy and DNS handling, cookies, downloads, and cache policy.

page_notes.content: Use lib/network/app_dio.dart, proxy.dart, cookie_jar.dart, cache.dart, images.dart, file_downloader.dart, and settings/network.dart. Explain cross-cutting impact on sources, downloads, auth, and reader image loading.

pages.title: UI Architecture & Shared Components
pages.purpose: Map the shared component layer and the older page-centric UI structure that still hosts much of the application.

page_notes.content: Use lib/components/ and lib/pages/. Clarify that shared components should remain UI-only, while new feature/runtime ownership is increasingly moving into lib/features.

pages.title: Comic Reader
pages.purpose: Document the main reader widget, read modes, gesture and chrome behavior, image provider usage, and page loading flow.

page_notes.content: Use lib/features/reader/presentation/, lib/foundation/image_provider/reader_image.dart, and lib/foundation/reader/. Distinguish UI lifecycle events from real persistence or route changes.

pages.title: Reader Lifecycle & Route Boundary
pages.purpose: Explain ReaderWithLoading, route dispatch authority, expectedReaderTabId semantics, retained-tab diagnostics, and parent/child lifecycle debugging.

page_notes.content: Use docs/tech-notes/routing-model-storage-diagnostics-ownership.md, docs/plans/2026-05-05-reader-cutover-local-reader-lifecycle-plan.md, lib/features/reader/presentation/diagnostics.dart, and reader trace tests. Make pending expectedReaderTabId semantics explicit.

pages.title: Comic Details & Shared Local-Remote Contract
pages.purpose: Describe the comic detail page stack, local/remote normalization, read/download/favorite actions, and the current shared contract direction.

page_notes.content: Use lib/pages/comic_details_page/, lib/features/comic_detail/data/, lib/foundation/comic_detail/, and comic detail tests. Emphasize the fork goal of a unified local/remote detail contract.

pages.title: Discovery, Search, Explore & Ranking
pages.purpose: Document browsing and search flows across explore, aggregated search, categories, rankings, and result pages.

page_notes.content: Use explore_page.dart, search_page.dart, aggregated_search_page.dart, categories_page.dart, ranking_page.dart, and search_result_page.dart. Explain which parts are page orchestration versus source-runtime integration.

pages.title: Home, Favorites, History & Image Favorites Pages
pages.purpose: Explain the current dashboard and collection pages and how they expose the state of migration/cutover work to end users.

page_notes.content: Use home_page.dart, favorites/, history_page.dart, image_favorites_page/, and related tests. Call out where these pages still read from bridges or compatibility surfaces.

pages.title: Reader Next Runtime & Cutover
pages.purpose: Describe the newer reader_next runtime, shell/open-controller flow, runtime models, bridges, and why it exists as a cutover track.

page_notes.content: Use lib/features/reader_next/runtime/, presentation/, bridge/, and infrastructure/. Present reader_next as an active migration/runtime lane, not as a fully finished replacement.

pages.title: Reader Next Preflight, Backfill & Importer
pages.purpose: Document the readiness gates, cutover preflights, explicit identity backfill, and importer/validation helpers tied to reader_next migration work.

page_notes.content: Use lib/features/reader_next/preflight/, backfill/, importer/, and docs/release/readernext-cutover-rollback-guide.md. Make clear which pieces are migration-time tooling versus steady-state runtime.

pages.title: Import, Export, WebDAV Sync & Backup
pages.purpose: Explain archive import/export, WebDAV sync, backup behavior, headless automation surfaces, and authority risks when moving data across formats.

page_notes.content: Use lib/utils/data_sync.dart, import/export utilities, lib/headless.dart, and related docs/tests. State which payloads are canonical data, which are compatibility imports, and which are preferences or caches.

pages.title: Docs, Plans & Release Notes
pages.purpose: Map the documentation structure so contributors know where to find architecture notes, active plans, done slices, and rollback guides.

page_notes.content: Use docs/tech-notes/, docs/dev-notes/, docs/plans/, docs/plans/done/, docs/plans/old/, and docs/release/. This page should function as a docs navigation guide for engineers.

pages.title: Tests & Guard Rails
pages.purpose: Describe the test layout and highlight the suites that enforce routing, persistence, authority, migration, and source-management contracts.

page_notes.content: Use test/foundation/, test/reader/, test/features/reader_next/, test/features/source_management/, test/pages/, and static guard tests. Explain how these suites act as architecture enforcement, not just regression checks.
